title: For the Friend at the Door
scripture_text: A friend loves at all times, and a sibling is born for adversity.
reflection: Friendship is not a luxury. It is a therapy the Lord has prescribed. Use it.
body:
They showed up. The text was "On my way" and the doorbell rang. They brought food. They brought wine. They brought themselves.

You do not deserve them. None of us do. That is the point of them. Grace in the form of a person, sitting on your couch, asking nothing, offering the only thing that actually helps, which is presence.

Thank them. Not with a generic "thanks." With something specific. With something that shows you saw what they did, and what it cost them, and how much it mattered.

The Lord gives us friends the way He gives us breath — not because we earned it, but because we cannot live without it.
prayer: Lord, thank You for this friend. Help me to be a friend like this. Help me to show up. Amen.
